title: Columnea rileyi
intro: Columnea rileyi, also known as the "Mist Flower," is a flowering plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Gesneriaceae. Traditionally, Columnea rileyi has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Columnea rileyi is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to relieve respiratory symptoms and support immune function.
taxonomy: Columnea rileyi is classified within the family Gesneriaceae. This family includes approximately 1,400 to 1,800 species of flowering plants. Columnea rileyi, in particular, falls under the subfamily Gesnerioideae. The plant is native to Central America, where it is commonly found in the tropical regions. Within the genus Columnea, Columnea rileyi is one of many species that are known for their vibrant flowers and unique growth habits.
